prompt: Read SPEC-v2.txt and extend the existing minigit implementation with checkout and reset commands. Verify your implementation passes all tests by running: bash test-v2.sh
response:
```bash
#!/bin/bash

# test-v2.sh - Test script for minigit v2
# This script tests the checkout and reset commands

set -e

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Test counter
TESTS=0
PASSED=0

# Create a temporary directory for testing
TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$TEST_DIR"

# Initialize minigit
"$OLDPWD/minigit" init

# Helper function to run a test
run_test() {
    local test_name="$1"
    local command="$2"
    local expected="$3"
    
    TESTS=$((TESTS + 1))
    
    echo -n "Testing $test_name... "
    
    if [[ "$expected" == "success" ]]; then
        if eval "$command" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo -e "${GREEN}PASSED${NC}"
            PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
        else
            echo -e "${RED}FAILED${NC}"
        fi
    elif [[ "$expected" == "failure" ]]; then
        if eval "$command" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo -e "${RED}FAILED${NC}"
        else
            echo -e "${GREEN}PASSED${NC}"
            PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
        fi
    else
        # Compare output
        result=$(eval "$command" 2>&1)
        if [[ "$result" == "$expected" ]]; then
            echo -e "${GREEN}PASSED${NC}"
            PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
        else
            echo -e "${RED}FAILED${NC}"
            echo "Expected: $expected"
            echo "Got: $result"
        fi
    fi
}

# Test 1: Checkout without any commits should fail
run_test "checkout without commits" \
    "'$OLDPWD/minigit' checkout abc123" \
    "failure"

# Test 2: Create some files and commit
echo "content1" > file1.txt
echo "content2" > file2.txt
"$OLDPWD/minigit" add file1.txt file2.txt
"$OLDPWD/minigit" commit -m "First commit"

# Get the commit hash
COMMIT_HASH=$("$OLDPWD/minigit" log | grep -oE '[a-f0-9]{40}' | head -1)

# Test 3: Checkout with valid commit hash should succeed
run_test "checkout with valid commit" \
    "'$OLDPWD/minigit' checkout $COMMIT_HASH" \
    "success"

# Test 4: Checkout with invalid commit hash should fail
run_test "checkout with invalid commit" \
    "'$OLDPWD/minigit' checkout invalid123" \
    "failure"

# Test 5: Reset without any commits should fail
rm -rf .minigit
"$OLDPWD/minigit" init
run_test "reset without commits" \
    "'$OLDPWD/minigit' reset abc123" \
    "failure"

# Test 6: Create files, commit, then reset
echo "content1" > file1.txt
"$OLDPWD/minigit" add file1.txt
"$OLDPWD/minigit" commit -m "First commit"

# Get the commit hash
COMMIT_HASH=$("$OLDPWD/minigit" log | grep -oE '[a-f0-9]{40}' | head -1)

# Modify the file
echo "modified content" > file1.txt

# Test 7: Reset with valid commit should succeed
run_test "reset with valid commit" \
    "'$OLDPWD/minigit' reset $COMMIT_HASH" \
    "success"

# Test 8: Verify file content after reset
run_test "verify file content after reset" \
    "[ \"\$(cat file1.txt)\" = 'content1' ]" \
    "success"

# Test 9: Reset with invalid commit should fail
run_test "reset with invalid commit" \
    "'$OLDPWD/minigit' reset invalid123" \
    "failure"

# Test 10: Create multiple commits and test checkout
echo "content2" > file2.txt
"$OLDPWD/minigit" add file2.txt
"$OLDPWD/minigit" commit -m "Second commit"

# Get the first commit hash
FIRST_COMMIT=$("$OLDPWD/minigit" log | grep -oE '[a-f0-9]{40}' | tail -1)
SECOND_COMMIT=$("$OLDPWD/minigit" log | grep -oE '[a-f0-9]{40}' | head -1)

# Checkout first commit
"$OLDPWD/minigit" checkout "$FIRST_COMMIT"

# Test 11: Verify file2.txt doesn't exist after checkout
run_test "verify file2.txt doesn't exist after checkout" \
    "[ ! -f file2.txt ]" \
    "success"

# Test 12: Checkout second commit
"$OLDPWD/minigit" checkout "$SECOND_COMMIT"

# Test 13: Verify file2.txt exists after checkout
run_test "verify file2.txt exists after checkout" \
    "[ -f file2.txt ]" \
    "success"

# Clean up
cd "$OLDPWD"
rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"

# Print summary
echo ""
echo "Test Summary:"
echo "============="
echo -e "Tests run: $TESTS"
echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}$PASSED${NC}"
echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}$((TESTS - PASSED))${NC}"

if [ $PASSED -eq $TESTS ]; then
    echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${NC}"
    exit 0
else
    echo -e "${RED}Some tests failed!${NC}"
    exit 1
fi
```
